infoBox.title: Website & API Uptime Monitoring
infoBox.description: Monitor your websites and APIs to ensure they're always accessible. Get instant alerts when your services go down and track uptime history over time.

fallback.title: An uptime monitor is used to:
checks: Check if websites or servers are online & responsive
Alert teams about downtime or performance issues
Monitor HTTP endpoints, pings, containers & ports
Track historical uptime and reliability trends

type.title: Type of check
type.description: Select the type of uptime monitor to create.
type.optionHttpsDescription: Use HTTPS to monitor your website or API endpoint.
type.optionHttpDescription: Use HTTP to monitor your website or API endpoint.
type.optionPingDescription: Use ICMP Ping to monitor if a server is online.
type.optionPortDescription: Monitor if a specific port on a server is open.

description.https: Enter the URL or IP to monitor (e.g., https://example.com/ or 192.168.1.100) and add a clear display name that appears on the dashboard.
description.http: Enter the URL or IP to monitor (e.g., https://example.com/ or 192.168.1.100) and add a clear display name that appears on the dashboard.
description.ping: Enter the IP address or hostname to ping (e.g., 192.168.1.100 or example.com) and add a clear display name that appears on the dashboard.
description.port: Enter the URL or IP of the server, the port number and a clear display name that appears on the dashboard.

rejectUnauthorized.title: Reject unauthorized
rejectUnauthorized.description: Disable to allow self-signed SSL certificates

infoBox.title: Website Performance Tracking
infoBox.description: Measure how fast your websites load and identify performance bottlenecks. Track Core Web Vitals and get actionable insights to improve user experience.

fallback.title: A PageSpeed monitor is used to:
checks: Report on the user experience of a page
Give suggestions on how the page can be improved

general.description: Enter the URL of the website you want to carry out PageSpeed tests on

infoBox.title: Infrastructure Monitoring
infoBox.description: Keep track of your servers' vital statistics including CPU usage, memory, disk space, and network performance. Receive alerts when resources run low.

checks: Track the performance of your servers
fallback.title: An infrastructure monitor is used to:

general.description: Enter the IP address or hostname of the server you want to monitor, along with a clear display name that appears on the dashboard.

thresholds.title: Thresholds for alerts
thresholds.description: Set percentage thresholds to trigger incidents

infoBox.title: Notification Channels
infoBox.description: Configure where you want to receive notifications when monitors detect issues. Connect email, Slack, Discord, webhooks, and other channels to stay informed.

checks: Alert teams about downtime or performance issues
Let engineers know when incidents happen
Keep administrators informed of system changes
fallback.title: Notification channles are used toto:

name.title: Channel name
name.description: A descriptive name for the notification channel

type.title: Channel type

email.title: Email configuration
email.description: Configure the email address where notifications will be sent

slack.title: Slack configuration
slack.description: Configure your Slack webhook URL. Create one in Slack: Settings & administration → Manage apps → Custom Integrations → Incoming Webhooks

discord.title: Discord configuration
discord.description: Configure your Discord webhook URL. Create one in Discord: Settings & administration → Manage apps → Custom Integrations → Incoming Webhooks

webhook.title: Webhook configuration
webhook.description: Configure the webhook URL endpoint where notifications will be sent

checks: Monitor and display the health of your services in real time
Track multiple services and share their status
Keep users informed about outages and performance

monitors.title: Monitors

infoBox.title: Scheduled Maintenance Periods
infoBox.description: Plan maintenance windows to temporarily pause monitoring checks. During maintenance, your monitors won't run checks or send notifications, preventing false alerts during planned downtime.

infoBox.title: About Maintenance Windows
infoBox.description: During maintenance windows, all monitoring is suspended for selected monitors. No network checks will be performed, preventing any status updates or notifications from being triggered. Your monitors will appear frozen at their last known status, and status pages will display a maintenance indicator. Once the maintenance window ends, monitoring automatically resumes, and alerts will trigger if issues are detected. Maintenance periods do not count against uptime calculations.

time.title: Start & end times
time.description: Set the start time and end time of the maintenance window

monitors.title: Monitors
monitors.description: Select the monitors to include in the maintenance window

checks: Stop sending alerts in maintenance windows
fallback.title: A maintenance window is used to:
